items.body_text: The World Cup is the latest proof of concept that it's possible to have grass fields in all 30 NFL stadiums, even the domes. But obstacles remain from keeping it full time.

items.title: NFL closes personal conduct review of WR Diggs
items.body_text: The NFL has closed its review of wide receiver Stefon Diggs after determining there was insufficient evidence of a personal conduct policy violation, a league official told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

items.title: Retired Ragnow: Body told me to stop playing
items.body_text: One year after announcing his abrupt retirement on social media, former Lions center Frank Ragnow broke his silence on the process and the failed physical that prevented his in-season return.

items.title: Broncos' Cooper arrested for alleged harassment
items.body_text: Broncos linebacker Jonathon Cooper was arrested Thursday after he allegedly violated a protection order against him, according to jail records.

items.title: Seahawks' SB LX rings salute fans, city, history
items.body_text: At a private ceremony Thursday night, the Seahawks received their Super Bowl LX rings, which feature diamonds, sapphires, team mantras and a tribute to the team's fans, among other elements.

items.title: Bowles backs Mayfield as long-term QB amid talks
items.body_text: Bucs coach Todd Bowles said he "absolutely" wants Baker Mayfield as his long-term quarterback and that he doesn't believe the lack of progress on a new deal has impacted him.

items.title: Sources: No NFL discipline for Chiefs coach Merrit...
items.body_text: The NFL has concluded its investigation of Kansas City Chiefs defensive backs coach Dave Merritt and found insufficient evidence of a violation of the personal conduct policy, sources told NFL Network's Mike Garafolo.

items.title: Ruggs denied parole nearly five years after crash
items.body_text: Ex-NFL player Henry Ruggs was denied parole nearly five years after killing a woman in a car crash in Las Vegas, the Nevada Board of Parole Commissioners ruled Thursday.

items.title: WR Brown marvels at Maye, 'a true leader of men'
items.body_text: A.J. Brown concluded his second week of practices with the Patriots on Thursday, later saying he has been impressed by Drake Maye: "He's a true leader of men. It's crazy to see at a young age."
